Military Mar 31, 2026

5 Fighter Jets With The Highest Top Speeds In Service Today

From the Mach 2.83 MiG-31 to the F-22, these are the 5 fastest fighter jets still in service—and why speed still matters in modern air combat.

Speed remains operationally critical for modern fighters despite advanced missiles, enabling tactical advantages in engagement initiation and escape scenarios.

JetBlue Hikes Checked Bags Fees, Cites "Rising Operating Costs"

JetBlue announces a hike checked bag fees, balancing the need to keep fares competitive with the reality of escalating operations costs.

JetBlue's fee increase signals broader carrier strategy to preserve margins without raising base fares in a price-sensitive market.

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts airlines from accident investigation to predictive safety modeling, requiring cultural transformation beyond compliance checkbox implementation.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris

For the second time in just over three months, a SpaceX Starlink satellite has generated debris from an apparent on-orbit malfunction.

Recurring Starlink anomalies raise concerns about collision cascade risks in already-congested low-earth orbit, potentially affecting aviation operations and satellite communications reliability.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta Slashes Domestic Boeing 767-400 Flights By 67% In Massive Schedule Shakeup

The airline will redeploy a significant portion of its 767-400ER fleet from domestic routes to services to and from Europe, affecting several routes.

Delta's shift reflects reduced domestic wide-body demand as airlines optimize fleet utilization by routing larger aircraft toward higher-yield international markets.

TSA workers receiving backpay as shutdown enters day 45, but those who received $1,000 gift cards from Tyler Perry are asked to return them.

TSA's extended shutdown created unexpected compliance complications when employee gifts became entangled with federal backpay distribution processes.

London Gatwick Confirms 9th New Airline In Massive 2026 Shakeup

Pegasus gives Gatwick a fresh Istanbul link as its surprising 2026 growth surges.

Gatwick's nine new carriers in 2026 signal post-capacity-expansion competition intensifying, with budget carriers like Pegasus targeting underserved routes to establish beachheads at previously constrained European hubs.

Rocket Lab wins German approval for Mynaric deal

Decision clears path for April closing after months of scrutiny over keeping key space technology in domestic hands...

German regulatory approval of Rocket Lab's Mynaric acquisition removes geopolitical barriers to closing, signaling growing acceptance of cross-border space industry consolidation despite national security concerns.

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

Reno-Tahoe's Mustang 9 departure from runways 17L/R requires heightened pilot attention due to procedure complexity that poses significant operational risk if misexecuted.

Why Airlines Don’t Always Use Their Newest Jets

Examining why the three US legacy carriers operate relatively old aircraft, and why American Airlines' fleet is yoinger than the rest.

Legacy carriers strategically defer new aircraft deployment to maximize existing asset utilization and manage capital expenditure timing amid fluctuating demand cycles.

Military Mar 30, 2026

China’s Y-9FQ Next Gen ASW Aircraft Intercepted for the First Time Over East China Sea

The Y-9FQ ‘High New 15’ made its debut at the Sep. 3, 2025, Victory Day parade in Beijing, and has now been intercepted for the first time by Japan. A Chinese People’s Liberation Army Naval Air Force (PLANAF) Y-9FQ maritime patrol and anti-submarine warfare aircraft was intercepted for the first...

China's newly unveiled Y-9FQ ASW aircraft has entered operational service faster than expected, demonstrating accelerated modernization of PLA maritime capabilities in contested regions.

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, demanding enhanced detection systems and lower-altitude operational protocols in congested areas.

Some airline routes are special because of the insatiable passenger demand they create, like Seoul to Jeju in South Korea, which is often ranked as the world’s busiest route. Others are special because they link two globally influential cities like New York and London. Then there’s Dallas to New...

Airline ancillary partnerships with local food vendors create differentiation on competitive routes and generate non-ticket revenue while enhancing passenger experience.

F-16s intercepted the general aviation aircraft near Mar-a-Lago on Sunday afternoon.

Sendoff for Artemis II Crew

From left to right, NASA astronauts Andre Douglas, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronauts Jenni Gibbons, NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen pose for a photo before the Artemis II crew proceed to a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas...

Artemis II's multinational crew composition reflects NASA's shift toward sustained international partnerships for lunar missions beyond initial exploration phases.

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact, presenting a more achievable path than relying solely on new aircraft technology.

See MDPI →

Air Algérie orders 10 Boeing 737-8s as fleet renewal accelerates

Air Algérie has ordered 10 Boeing 737 MAX 8 aircraft, with deliveries set to begin in summer 2026 as the…...

North African carriers are increasingly embracing MAX aircraft despite prior regional hesitation, signaling growing confidence in Boeing's re-certified platform among emerging market operators.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer

MILAN — The European Space Agency has launched the first two satellites of the Celeste in-orbit demonstration mission from New Zealand aboard a Rocket Lab Electron launcher on March 28. The two satellites were launched at 10:14 CET and separated from the launcher about an hour later into a...

ESA's Celeste constellation validates a backup GPS alternative for aviation through low-Earth orbit satellites, reducing European dependence on U.S. systems.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission

SpaceX launched the latest in its series of dedicated rideshare missions March 30, delivering more than 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

SpaceX's rideshare model continues validating commercial small-satellite economics by achieving over 100 payloads per mission, establishing scalable launch accessibility for emerging space operators.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Merlin Labs sees incremental path to pilot-free passenger flights

US start-up Merlin Labs is eyeing civil applications for its AI-powered, defense-grade Merlin Pilot autonomous flight control system and says there is “no hard ceiling” for the size of aircraft its technology could eventually be installed on. Describing fully uncrewed civil flight as “a very long...

Merlin Labs' defense-derived autonomous system could scale to large aircraft, positioning AI automation as a regulatory-phased transition rather than an all-or-nothing capability leap.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Bars U.S. Iran-Related Military Flights From Its Airspace

Madrid expands earlier restrictions on base access for U.S. military flights.

Spain's escalating airspace restrictions signal NATO allies' willingness to limit U.S. military operations over geopolitical concerns, potentially fragmenting transatlantic military air corridors.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit

Chinese firm CAS Space successfully reached orbit with the first launch of its Kinetica-2 launch vehicle Monday, with a prototype cargo spacecraft among the payloads.

China's commercial launch capability expands with a new domestic rocket platform, reducing reliance on foreign providers for orbital access.

Ground stop affected Washington-area airports Friday for the second time in two weeks.

Repeated TRACON evacuations signal potential infrastructure vulnerabilities in one of the nation's busiest airspace sectors, warranting urgent systemic review.

JFK's New Terminal One is establishing dedicated accessibility infrastructure with specialized staffing, signaling a competitive shift in passenger experience differentiation among major US airports.

FBI says there is no continuing threat after passenger’s verbal bomb-threat.
